steps.title: Set up and simplify the area equation
steps.content:
Use the formula for the area of a triangle: $A = \frac{1}{2} \times \text{base} \times \text{height}$

Let $h$ = height. The base is 12 cm greater, so base $= h + 12$.

Substitute into the area formula:
$$270 = \frac{1}{2}(h + 12) \cdot h$$

Multiply both sides by 2 to clear the fraction:
$$540 = h^2 + 12h$$
$$h^2 + 12h - 540 = 0$$

steps.title: Backsolve the answer choices
steps.content:
Since the choices are simple integers, test them directly instead of using the quadratic formula.

Backsolving — testing choice C (30):
If $h = 30$, base $= 42$, and area $= \frac{1}{2}(42)(30) = 630 \neq 270$. Fails.

Backsolving — testing choice B (18):
If $h = 18$, base $= 30$, and area $= \frac{1}{2}(30)(18) = 270$. ✓

That matches choice B — B is correct.
